# LLM-as-Database — Parametric Knowledge Editing as a Storage Substrate

<!-- BEGIN GENERATED:program-state -->
**📍 PROGRAM STATE (updated 2026-06-26 (AnyEdit advisor FIX-FIRST resolved by token-alignment gate; prereg next))** — _auto-generated from `docs/program_state.json` — DO NOT edit between the markers; run `python3 tools/render_state.py --write`._

- **North star:** F1 — prove/falsify the 'LLM-as-Database' spec is implementable BEFORE it is built; deliver a ready / not-ready-with-conditions determination. Falsification-first.
- **Latest:** **DEPLOYMENT TARGET FIXED (operator): `local Intel CPU + batch writes` → F1 re-scoped + first on-target falsifier fired.** Re-scope (advisor-checked for over-rotation): C4/§8.7/D1-incremental DROP from the critical path (incremental-only); **C1 REFRAMES** to batch-genesis-cleanliness-at-scale (gated on target fact-count, NOT deleted); **C10 multi-token values ELEVATED**. **C10 (D-C10-1, CORPUS/35) = FALSIFIER FIRED:** 3-arm novel-insert at the N≤100 batch path (binding = held-out-paraphrase full-sequence) — single-token 97.2% & prior-COHERENT multi-token 97.2% robust, but arbitrary INCOHERENT multi-token values 36.1% (first-token 70.8%; conditional P(full|first)=0.51 vs coherent 1.00). The edit FITS the trained prompt (95.8%) but does NOT GENERALIZE to paraphrases → the multi-token instance of the R13/R5 trained-prompt-parrot axis. **C10 = the FIRST condition both on the critical path for the fixed target AND failing → OPEN MUST-FIX; named fix = AnyEdit per-token editing.** 3 advisor gates + Perplexity cross-family; scoped 3B/band[4-8]/AlphaEdit/N≤100/1-seed/HF-fp16 (single-token + prior-coherent values already robust). F1 net readiness UNCHANGED (NOT-READY-WITH-CONDITIONS) but the target now has a concrete blocker with a known fix to try. ⮕ FOLLOW-UP (c10c, D-C10b-diverse): tested whether paraphrase-diverse training rescues the incoherent failure — it does NOT cleanly (CANON+PTRAIN degraded everything via 3×-request interference → uninformative); replicates C10 (38.9≈36.1). The wall is GENERALIZATION at the continuation position, cleanly localized (single-token 97% → first-of-multi 82% → continuation collapses) — a reasonable mechanistic case FOR the AnyEdit fix (canon=100% is a teacher-forcing artifact, not 'already solved'). AnyEdit port = OPERATOR-GATED (multi-day; mechanistic case but payoff empirically unproven; PORT jianghoucheng/AnyEdit, don't reimplement). Alternative: accept C10 as a documented value-class limitation (single-token + prior-coherent values work today). ⮕ SEVERITY BOUND (spec-grounded, advisor-corrected — NOT a downgrade): §7.1/D1 (LLM=semantics, Git=syntax) removes the SYNTACTIC subset (paths/identifiers/exact strings → Git) from the in-weight fragility surface, BUT the fragility axis is prior-coherence (orthogonal to syntax) → the residual = project-coined multi-word SEMANTIC named entities (§7.2 domain_concept) with no prior = the non-prior-coherent fragile class, plausibly ENRICHED (the value-prop of an in-weight project KB is exactly the base-model-unknown facts). C10 stays OPEN/CONDITIONAL: bites iff CORE in-weight facts include project-novel multi-word semantic values (a project KB likely has). Both the AnyEdit port AND an empirical residual-test are OPERATOR-GATED.

**⮕ RESIDUAL-TEST DONE 2026-06-26 (D-C10b-residual, CORPUS/36 — operator's Option B): RESIDUAL FRAGILE.** 7-arm novel-insert (same 24 fictional subjects): single 98.6% & coherent-bigram 98.6% robust, but **realistic project-coined multi-word SEMANTIC values express at only 19–31% held-out-paraphrase full-seq** (RANGE [A7 coined-coined 19.4%, A6 coined+real-noun 30.6%]) — ≪ the 85% gate, below the 2-tok floor (45.8%) AND the original C10 floor (36%) because they tokenize LONG (4–5.5 tok). **→ the severity-bound prediction is empirically confirmed: the in-weight value class a real project KB holds IS fragile, not a corner case.** Two precise claims: (1) C10 CONDITION CONFIRMED-and-WORSENED; (2) dominant MECHANISM = a DISTINCT multi-token length-realization wall, NOT C10's fit-then-fail parrot — realistic arms fail to even FIT the canonical prompt (canon 37–46% vs floor 95.8%). z-probe (advisor cheap-check): compute_z reaches 0.99 target-prob for ALL classes @25≈100 steps → NOT a *compute_z* knob (z-target achievable at 0.99); bottleneck RELOCATES to W-realization, whose knobs (band/layer-count/edit-strength) are UNTESTED → option A (AnyEdit) CONSISTENT-not-proven-necessary; cheap resolver = wider/later band BEFORE a port. Continuation prior-coherence mildly protective per-tok (60–72% vs 46%) but length-overwhelmed. Advisor before build+verdict; Perplexity cross-family on interpretation. F1 net readiness UNCHANGED. Scope 3B/band[4-8]/N≤24/1-seed; NOT promotable.

**⮕ BAND-KNOB DONE 2026-06-26 (D-C10e-bandknob ⟨D-C10e-bandknob@82b491dc⟩, CORPUS/37): NO MATERIAL KNOB RESCUE.** C10e tested the cheapest W-realization knob before AnyEdit: baseline `[4,8]` vs later `[8,12]` on the hard A7 coined-coined arm, with A1/A2 controls. Later band **does NOT rescue held-out behavioral readout**: A7 `para_full` worsens **13.9 to 5.6** (-8.3pp), `para_first` worsens **75.0 to 33.3**, and teacher-forced continuation worsens **60.2 to 34.0**. The canonical trained-prompt diagnostic improves only **29.2 to 45.8** (+16.6pp), still below the pre-registered rescue threshold and below 60; `para_full` remains binding. A1/A2 controls remain high (97.2 / 91.7 later), so the failure is within-tested-arms specific to long no-prior coined-coined values. Scope 3B/N=24/1-seed/HF-fp16; NOT promotable. This eliminates the pure later-band explanation only; wider `[4,12]`, layer-count/edit-strength, and AnyEdit remain untested/consistent-not-proven-necessary. F1 readiness unchanged.

**⮕ WIDENED-BAND DONE 2026-06-26 (D-C10f-band412 ⟨D-C10f-band412@d93d6a19⟩, CORPUS/38): NO MATERIAL KNOB RESCUE.** C10f tested the next cheap W-realization knob before AnyEdit: baseline `[4,8]` vs widened `[4,12]` on the hard A7 coined-coined arm, with A1/A2 controls. The widened recipe **does NOT rescue held-out behavioral readout**: A7 `para_full` worsens **11.1 to 6.9** (-4.2pp), `para_first` worsens **72.2 to 37.5**, and teacher-forced continuation worsens **56.2 to 40.1**. Canonical trained-prompt fit improves only **29.2 to 41.7** (+12.5pp), below the W-realization-only threshold and far below usability. A1/A2 controls remain above floor but degrade **100.0 to 90.3**. Scope 3B/N=24/1-seed/HF-fp16; NOT promotable. This eliminates this `[4,12]` band-widening recipe only; edit-strength/layer-count and AnyEdit remain untested/consistent-not-proven-necessary. F1 readiness unchanged.

**⮕ STRENGTH/LAYER SWEEP DONE 2026-06-26 (D-C10g-strengthlayers ⟨D-C10g-strengthlayers@d691acab⟩, CORPUS/39): NO BEHAVIORAL RESCUE WITH ONE NON-VIABLE TRADEOFF.** C10g tested the final bounded pre-AnyEdit MEMIT knob pass: strength150 `[4,12]`, lowcov2500 `[4,12]`, and deep `[4..12,18..22]` on hard A7 with A1/A2 controls. No candidate reached behavioral lead: baseline A7 `para_full` 20.8 (15/72); strength150 13.9 (10/72) with clean controls; lowcov2500 5.6 (4/72) with clean controls despite canon 45.8; deep canon 58.3 but A7 `para_full` 0/72 and controls collapse A1 37.5/A2 22.2. LAW#5 all pass. This closes cheap MEMIT band/strength/layer-count route for this scoped A7/Qwen2.5-3B run only; does not prove AnyEdit necessary or MEMIT generally exhausted. F1 unchanged.

**⮕ ANYEDIT PRE-COMMIT TRIAGE DONE 2026-06-26 (D-C10h-anyedit-triage): PROCEED-TO-VIABILITY-GATED PILOT DESIGN; NOT EVIDENCE.** Independent advisor-review returned **PROCEED** with a hard first gate: code-level viability audit before harness authoring. Implementation triage of the official repos found AnyEdit is the smaller first port target: `jianghoucheng/AnyEdit` ships `AlphaEdit_ARE/` + `MEMIT_ARE/`, Qwen2.5-7B hparams, and an autoregressive per-token/window loop, but assumes old deps (`transformers==4.23.1`) + A100-class headroom and uses Qwen hparams (`lm_head`) that do not directly match the local Qwen2.5-3B/transformers==4.51.0 harness (`model.embed_tokens`, local clamp/cov settings). Therefore the next scientific move is NOT running upstream as-is; it is a transplant preserving local MEMIT primitives and LAW#5 inertness, then a minimal A7 + A1/A2 held-out full-sequence pilot. FABLE is real and relevant but heavier/more invasive (EasyEdit/UnKE-style layer-parameter optimization with preservation examples), so it is a fallback if AnyEdit is infeasible or fails cleanly. EV-6 broad AnyEdit-neighborhood review confirms this path but upgrades the gate: `DCDmllm/AnyEdit` and RefEdit are image-editing name collisions; LLM AnyEdit remains `jianghoucheng/AnyEdit`; μKE/AnyEdit++ make fixed-window dependency logging mandatory; COIN makes context-reliance mandatory; SUIT is a cleaner over-edit fallback; NeuralDB/SCR increase pressure on the side-store/gated-overlay alternative. This review strengthens AnyEdit as the best first C10 code target, but does not prove it necessary or sufficient; accept-bounded remains the alternative.

**⮕ ANYEDIT VIABILITY AUDIT DONE 2026-06-26 (D-C10h-anyedit-viability; docs/C10_ANYEDIT_VIABILITY_AUDIT.md): PROCEED-TO-PREREG/ADVISOR, BUT DEFAULT WINDOW IS INVALID AS PRIMARY C10 TEST.** Code-level audit confirms the official LLM AnyEdit repo is the correct target and a harness-side transplant is plausible without editing the local MEMIT engine or downgrading dependencies. Critical correction: upstream `window_size=50` creates only one window for C10 A7 values because they tokenize to 4-6 Qwen2.5 tokens (mean 5.5); a C10 per-token/window rescue must preregister a small-window primary condition (likely `window_size=1`) plus any default-window run only as diagnostic. External advisor-review returned FIX-FIRST: prove standalone `answer` tokenization matches the `question + answer` continuation suffix before prereg/harness. The no-GPU token-alignment gate now passes for current A7 stimuli (24/24 suffix alignment with leading-space answers; `window_size=50` still one window), so prereg may proceed only with this gate embedded as an abort condition.
- **F1 status:** DETERMINATION DRAFTED 2026-06-24 (`docs/F1_DETERMINATION.md`) = **NOT-READY-WITH-CONDITIONS** (draft-with-open-conditions, NOT a clean close; shippable as the honest current state). Deployment data-path spine PROVEN-FOR-SCOPE (recipe→A1 batch-clean→B3 Q4_K_M→E1·A CPU-serve; 3B/N≤100/band[4-8]/single-batch/1-seed/fictional). Architecture = scope-keyed COMPACTION-BOUNDED HYBRID (D-B3N-1), in-weight a serving copy per the verified §11.2/D42 structural finding (no class weights-authoritative). C1/C2 closing negative would move the verdict toward not-implementable-as-specified. Closure-path lead = UNGATED `tools/power.py` pool-sizing.

<!-- END GENERATED:program-state -->

A falsification-first research program testing whether facts can be **stored in, retrieved from, governed within, and deployed from an LLM's weights** — treating a transformer as a queryable database rather than an opaque generator. Editing is done with the MEMIT/AlphaEdit family (closed-form `down_proj` null-space solves); the program stress-tests a written spec (`research_and_specs/llm-as-database-v1_2-integrated-spec.md`) toward implementation-readiness.

This repo is organized for a research audience: every headline claim resolves to a raw artifact, every experiment is pre-registered and re-runnable, and caveats are kept flush with results. It follows the **ML Reproducibility Checklist** (Pineau et al.), **"Good enough practices in scientific computing"** (Wilson et al.), **Model-Cards** disclosure norms (Mitchell et al.), and **FAIR** principles.

> **North star (never drift):** the goal is the **F1 spec-implementation-readiness determination** for the LLM-as-Database thesis — *is it buildable?* Everything serves F1. Operating discipline (goal, context-triggers, failure-thinking, tool thresholds) is in **[`DISCIPLINE.md`](./DISCIPLINE.md)**.
>
> **New here? Read in this order:** this README → `REPRODUCIBILITY.md` → `docs/EXPERIMENT_REGISTRY.md` → `CORPUS/README.md` (the evidence) → `research_and_specs/…spec.md` (the contract). For the live roadmap and "what's next," see `EXPERIMENT_RUNBOOK.md` §0.3.

---

## Headline findings (what is PROVEN / PARTIAL / FALSIFIED)

| Area | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **Governance** (in-pipeline write, 2PC, security, validation) | ✅ PROVEN-FOR-SCOPE (design-viability — these test our own control flow, not nature; weaker-than-empirical) | `CORPUS/07–12` (CP1–CP3, G1–G3) |
| **Single/few-fact, same-entity store** | ✅ VALIDATED (Qwen2.5; model- & size-dependent — Qwen2.5-7B local, GPT-J not) | Phase-1, `docs/framework_findings/` |
| **Cross-entity store at scale (sequential)** | ❌ FALSIFIED — held-out same-relation reads corrupt 100→41.7% with N (the first empirical falsifier) | `CORPUS/13` (G6.1) |
| **Batch / Genesis write at scale** | ✅ ELIMINATES the corruption at 3B (flat 100→100→100%) | `CORPUS/14` (A1) |
| **In-solve sentinels / K_S refresh** | 🟡 PARTIAL / ruled-out (halve but don't arrest the sequential decline; staleness not the cause) | `CORPUS/15–16` (A2, A2b) |
| **Real Q4_K_M quantization survival** | ✅ PASS — edits survive deployment quantization (edited 100% vs native 97.4%) | `CORPUS/17` (B3) |
| **CPU deployment serving** | ✅ Claim A PASS via **llama.cpp** (~8–13 tok/s) · ❌ Claim B FALSIFIED via **LARQL** (drops Qwen2.5 attn bias; A7 causal proof) | `CORPUS/18` (E1) |
| **Size-density (3B→7B)** | 🟡 PARTIAL — batch-clean does not *fully* replicate at 7B (100→91.7%) | `CORPUS/19` (B1) |
| **Cross-entity mechanism (keying)** | ⛔ relation-keying PRUNED; same-relation key collinearity is U-shaped in depth (min L8–12) — a measured mechanism + a spec-C15 tension | `CORPUS/20` (C2) |

**Net state:** the **batch-rebuild deployment path** is clean at 3B and survives Q4_K_M; CPU serving works via llama.cpp. Open frontier: whether in-weight storage (L2) is even required vs. retrieval (L1) + an external query index; the capacity law (D1 — structural DONE+model-general; §8.7 numeric guardrail set conservative at per-relation **k≤1** on 3B, D-D1-2 ⟨D-D1-2@e023d8d2⟩, cross-model transfer open); and whether the edit-validated family (Qwen2.5) should move to the LARQL-servable family (Qwen3). See `docs/HYPOTHESIS_REGISTER_2026-06-18.md`.

```

docs/                   ← EXPERIMENT_REGISTRY, HYPOTHESIS_REGISTER, framework_findings/, session_summaries/, runbooks/
experiments/            ← re-runnable code by track: governance/ scale/ track_a/ track_b/ track_c/ deployment/
configs/                ← hparams/ screens/ probes/
results/   logs/        ← structured result JSONs · run logs
archive/                ← frozen historical scripts (s-series), notebooks, stale subdirs
memory_mirror/          ← durable cross-session learnings
— infrastructure (large, unmoved) —
memit_dry_run/ (MEMIT engine)  external_prior_art/ (larql, BetaEdit)  hf_cache/  covariance_caches/
llama_cpp_src/  b3_edited_qwen3b/  b3_q4km.vindex/  *.gguf  stage_1_sect/  architecture_profile/
```

## Quickstart / reproduce

```bash
export LLMDB_ROOT=/workspace          # default; set if the repo lives elsewhere
# a no-GPU sanity check (re-derives the B3 quantization verdict from stored predictions):
python experiments/track_b/b3_verdict.py
# a GPU mechanism probe (~1 min: loads Qwen2.5-3B, prints the key-collinearity depth map):
python experiments/track_c/c2_key_collinearity.py
```
Every experiment's exact command, inputs, and expected artifact are in **`REPRODUCIBILITY.md`** and **`docs/EXPERIMENT_REGISTRY.md`**.

## Scope & honest caveats (read before citing)
- **Model/range:** primarily Qwen2.5-3B (some 7B, Qwen3-0.6B); N ≤ 100 edits; Q4_K_M only; mostly single-seed, single write-ordering. Counterfactual-reassignment edits (not insertion/deletion).
- **Governance (CP1–G3) is design-viability**, not empirical falsification — it verifies our code implements the spec's contracts, a weaker category (see `CORPUS/03` and `[[prototype-tautology-trap]]`).
- **A measurement confound runs through the edit metrics:** MEMIT's `compute_z` inflates edited-fact margins vs native (`CORPUS/17`) — read retention numbers with that in mind.
- Reproducibility was preserved across a 2026-06-18 repo reorganization via a `LLMDB_ROOT` path convention; pre-reorg snapshot at `/root/migration_backup/`.
